breadcrumb: Technical Specification of Paint Coatings for BPCL Refineries > ANNEXURE 2 Paint Performance Guarantee Requirements > 5 Submission of Guarantees:
text: 5 Submission of Guarantees:

breadcrumb: Technical Specification of Paint Coatings for BPCL Refineries > ANNEXURE 2 Paint Performance Guarantee Requirements > 5 Submission of Guarantees:
text:
All guarantees must be submitted to BPCL in the standard format provided within
this specification. The guarantee shall comprise two components:
i) Performance Guarantee: The painting Contractor shall provide a bank
guarantee valid for two years from the date of job completion. This guarantee
must be equivalent to 10% of the total contract value for the painting work and
will serve as the retention security for the initial two-year period.
ii) Corporate Guarantee : For the remaining guarantee period, a corporate
guarantee must be submitted. This corporate guarantee must be jointly
signed by both the painting Contractor and the paint manufacturer, based on
a formal agreement between the two parties.
iii) These guarantees must be submitted either before the commencement of the
painting job or immediately after its completion. Payment will be released only
upon submission of the required guarantees.

breadcrumb: Technical Specification of Paint Coatings for BPCL Refineries > ANNEXURE 2 Paint Performance Guarantee Requirements > 6 Joint Inspections and Maintenance During Guarantee Period:
text: 6 Joint Inspections and Maintenance During Guarantee Period:

breadcrumb: Technical Specification of Paint Coatings for BPCL Refineries > ANNEXURE 2 Paint Performance Guarantee Requirements > 6 Joint Inspections and Maintenance During Guarantee Period:
text:
During the guarantee period, the paint manufacturer shall conduct joint
inspections with BPCL engineers as follows:
• Every six months during the first two years
• Annually thereafter for the remaining guarantee period
All necessary repairs identified during these inspections shall be carried out by
the paint manufacturer at their own cost. The extent of corrosion and coating
performance shall be jointly monitored and documented during these inspections.

text: ANNEXURE 5 Procedure for Measurement of Dry Film Coating Thickness

breadcrumb: Technical Specification of Paint Coatings for BPCL Refineries > ANNEXURE 5 Procedure for Measurement of Dry Film Coating Thickness
text: Thickness

breadcrumb: Technical Specification of Paint Coatings for BPCL Refineries > ANNEXURE 5 Procedure for Measurement of Dry Film Coating Thickness > Definitions
text: Definitions

breadcrumb: Technical Specification of Paint Coatings for BPCL Refineries > ANNEXURE 5 Procedure for Measurement of Dry Film Coating Thickness > Definitions
text:
DFT – Dry Film Thickness
Gauge Reading – A single DFT reading at one point.
Spot – A circle of 4 cm diameter
Spot Measurement - Average of acceptable three-gauge readings within a spot (Circle
of 4 cm diameter)

breadcrumb: Technical Specification of Paint Coatings for BPCL Refineries > ANNEXURE 5 Procedure for Measurement of Dry Film Coating Thickness > Spot Measurement Procedure
text: Spot Measurement Procedure

breadcrumb: Technical Specification of Paint Coatings for BPCL Refineries > ANNEXURE 5 Procedure for Measurement of Dry Film Coating Thickness > Spot Measurement Procedure
text:
Minimum of three (3) gauge readings shall be made for each spot measurement of either
the substrate or the coating. Repeated gauge readings, even at points close together,
may differ due to small surface irregularities of the coating and the substrate. For each
new gauge reading move the probe to a new location within 4 cm diameter circle defining
the spot. Discard any unusually high or low reading that cannot be repeated consistently.
Take the average of the acceptable gauge readings as the spot measurement.

breadcrumb: Technical Specification of Paint Coatings for BPCL Refineries > ANNEXURE 5 Procedure for Measurement of Dry Film Coating Thickness > Spot Measurement Procedure
text: Required Number of Measurements for Conformance to a Thickness Specification. Number of Measurements: Make five (5) spot measurements spaced randomly over each 10 m 2 (100 ft 2 ) area to be measured. No. of spot measurement beyond five for an area of 10 m 2 can be increased as decided by BPCL. The five spot measurements shall be made for each 10 m 2 ( 100 ft 2 ) of area as follows: a. For structures not exceeding 30 m 2 (300 ft 2 ) in area, each 10 sq. m 2 (100 ft 2 ) area shall be measured. b. For structures from 30 m 2 to 100 m 2 (1000 ft 2 ) in area, three 10 m 2 (100 ft 2 ) areas shall be randomly selected and measured. c. For structures exceeding 100 m 2 (1000 ft 2 ) in area, the first 10 m 2 (1000 ft 2 ) shall be measured as stated in (b) above and for increment thereof or each additional 100 m 2 (1000 ft 2 ) of area, one 10 m 2 (1000 ft 2 ) area shall be randomly selected and measured. d. If the dry film thickness for any 10 m 2 (100 ft 2 ) area as mentioned in (b) & (c) above is not in compliance with the requirements of (e) & (f) as mentioned below, then additional measurements must be made to isolate the

text: for any 10 m 2 (100 ft 2 ) area as mentioned in (b) & (c) above is not in compliance with the requirements of (e) & (f) as mentioned below, then additional measurements must be made to isolate the nonconforming area.

breadcrumb: Technical Specification of Paint Coatings for BPCL Refineries > ANNEXURE 5 Procedure for Measurement of Dry Film Coating Thickness > Spot Measurement Procedure
text: Specifying Thickness: Both a maximum and a minimum DFT should be specified for the coating. If a maximum thickness value is not explicitly specified, the specified thickness shall be minimum. a. Minimum Thickness: The average of five spot measurements for each 10 m 2 (100 ft 2 ) area shall not be less than the specified minimum DFT. The average of three- gauge readings at any single spot measurement in any 10 m 2 (100 ft 2 ) area shall not be less than 80% of the specified minimum DFT. If the average of five spot measurements for a given 10 m 2 (100 ft 2 ) area meets or exceeds the specified minimum DFT and any spot measurement does not meet 80% of the specified minimum DFT, additional spot measurements may be made to define the non- conforming areas. b. Maximum Thickness: The average of five spot measurements for each 10 m 2 (100 ft 2 ) area shall not be more than the specified maximum DFT. No single spot measurement in any 10 m 2 (100 ft 2 ) area shall be more than 120% of the specified minimum DFT. If the average of the five spot measurements of a given 10 m 2 (100 ft 2 ) area meets or falls below the specified maximum DFT, but one or more spot measurements is more than 120% of the specified minimum DFT, additional spot measurements may be made
